"Benchmark task: react-state-bug-fix
Repository: https://github.com/honojs/hono.git

Fix the state update bug in bench-targets/react-state. Read bench-targets/react-state/src/useCartState.mjs, bench-targets/react-state/src/CartPanel.jsx, and bench-targets/react-state/useCartState.test.mjs before editing. Keep the change minimal and preserve existing exports. The targeted verification command is node --test bench-targets/react-state/useCartState.test.mjs.

Constraints:
- Do not ask the user to write prompts or inspect files manually.
- Prefer the smallest safe code change when edits are required.
- Include exact file paths, commands run, and verification status in the final answer."
I need to start by reading the three files mentioned to understand the bug before making any changes.

state.title: bench-targets/react-state/useCartState.test.mjs

parts.text: I've read the three files and can now identify the bug. In useCartState.mjs, line 7-8 shows `next.items.push(item)` which mutates the original state. The test expects `state.items` to remain unchanged (empty array). I need to fix this by cloning the items array before modifying it.

The bug is clear: in `useCartState.mjs` line 7, `next.items.push(item)` mutates the original `state.items` array instead of creating a shallow copy, which breaks strict React immutability. The test expects `state.items` to remain unchanged.
